title: Tabora Municipal Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2002
title_en: Tabora Municipal Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2002

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: This instrument may be cited as the Tabora Municipal Council ( Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2002 and shall come into operation on the date of its publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: This Instrument shall apply throughout the jurisdiction of the Tabora Municipal Council .

provisions.title: 3 Interpretation
provisions.content: In the Instrument unless the context otherwise requires: " Act " means the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act, 1982; " Health Centre Committee " means a committee established under section 18 of this Instrument; " Health Centre Management Team " means a team of head of sections or units of a Health Centre ; " Ward Development Committee " means a committee established under Section 15 of the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act ; " Dispensary Committee " means a committee established under Section 25 (1) of this Instrument; " Council Health Management Team " means a technical health managerial organ of the District established under section 13 of this Instrument; " Dispensary " means a primary health care facility, which offers outpatient services, including maternal and child health services and may have up to four beds for observation of patients for not more than 24 hours; " District " means Tabora Municipal Council ; " Health Centre " means a health facility usually located at the Ward levels offering outpatient and inpatient health care services including maternal and child health services with a bed capacity of about 15 beds; " Hospital " means a level 1 - hospital establishment in the City/Municipal/Town empowered to supervise all health services in the District ; " Designated Hospital " means a voluntary agency level 1 - hospital establishment empowered to act as a City/Municipal/Town hospital on contractual agreement with the Ministry; " Health Facility " means and includes a hospital, health centre, medical and dental clinics, dispensary, pharmacy, health laboratory, diagnostic centre, maternity home and nursing home; and such other facilities established or organised for purposes of delivering health services; " Medical Officer of Health " means a medical practitioner who is the of-ficer in charge of health services in the City/Municipal/Town; " Public health facility " means a health facility that is mainly owned and rnanaged by the Government to address people's needs; " Minister " means the Minister responsible for Local Governmentand Regional Administration; " Non Governmental Organisation (NGO) Health Facility " means a health facility owned, financed and run by an organisation or an association. Normally charitable non profit making with the aim of complimenting public or private services and operates within laid down regulations of the Government; " Private Health facility " means a health facility owned and managed by an individual or corporate body mainly for profit; " Board " means the Council Health Service Board established under section 4(1) of this Instrument; " Council " means the City/Municipal/Town Council established under section 5 of the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act 1982; " Regional Health Management Team " means a managerial organ established by the Ministry of Health; " Ward Health Committee " means a Committee established under section 16 of this Instrument; " Ward Health Committee " means a Committee established under section 32 of this Instrument. [ Act No.8 of 1982 ]

provisions.title: Establishment of the Board
provisions.content: (1) There is hereby established a Board , to be known as the Council Health Service Board . (2) The officers of the Board shall be within the respective City/Municipal/Town.

provisions.title: Compostion of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall be composed of the following members, namely:— (a) Four community service users of health facility of whom at least two shall be female; (b) One representative each from a non profit voluntary agency and private for profit health care facility, appointed by the council from amongst health care facilities which have entered into a service agreement with the Board ; (c) The Chairman of the Council Social Services Committee; (d) The Council Planning Officer; (e) The District Medical Officer who shall be the Secretary to the Board ; (f) One representative from the hospital; and (g) One representative from the Regional Health Management Team . (2) The Chairman shall be elected from amongst members specified under paragraphs (a), (b) and (c) of subsection (1) of this section. (3) Members specified under paragraphs (a) , (b) , and (c) other than members specified under paragraphs (d) , (e) , (f) and (g) of subsection (1) , shall have voting rights in any decision of the Board.

provisions.title: Tenure of office and mode of selection
provisions.content: (1) Members of the Board shall hold office for a period of three years from the date of their nomination and cease to be a member after expiry of that period, but shall be eligible for re-nomination. (2) At the end of the tenure of once of the current Board , the Council Director shall announce to the public the open vacancy, which require competent personnel to all them by filling special application form and submit them to the Council . (3) if any vacancy occurs in the membership of the Board by the reason of death, resignation, permanent incapacity or any other reasons another person shall be nominated by the Board to fill up the vacancy and person nominated shall hold office for the remaining term provided that nothing in this section shall be constructed as allowing to nominate another person if the remaining period is less than six months. (4) The person so nominated as per subsection (3) above and eligible for re-election. (5) If the Board fails to fulfill its expected obligations as stipulated by the relevant Council 's Instruments the Council shall reprimand or advise the Minister responsible for Regional Administration and Local Government to dissolve the Board and instruct the council to arrange for an immediate selection. (6) Selection of applicants shall be made by the Full Council through the Social Services Committee after being scrutinized by the Council Management Team. (7) The Board shall be approved by the full Council and inaugurated under the witness of the District Commissioner.

provisions.title: Meetings of Council Health Service Board
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provision of supsection (2) of this section, the Board shall ordinarily meet quarterly in every calendar year and the Chairman shall convene all meetings of the Board . (2) The Chairman may convene a special extra-ordinary meeting of the Board on a request in writing signed by not less than one third of the total number of members of the Board when the need arises.

provisions.title: Quorum and voting at meeting
provisions.content: (1) A one-half of the total number of members in office shall constitute a quorum at any meeting of the Board . (2) A decision of the majority of members present and voting at a meeting the Board shall be deemed to be a decision of the Board ; (3) Secret votes system shall be used in Board 's meetings. (4) Every member of the Board shall have on vote and in event of equality of votes, the Chairman of the meeting shall have a second or casting vote in addition to his deliberative vote.

provisions.title: Qualifications of members
provisions.content: (1) The qualification of Board members shall base on three aspects as provided hereunder:— (a) Shall be a Tanzania citizen; (b) for representative of NGOs and Voluntary Agency, the question of citizenship shall not apply; (c) he has attained the age of 25 years and not older than sixty five years, provided that the subject is of sound body and mind; and (2) A councilor or an employee of the Council , whether elected or not, save for Chairman of Social Services Committee shall not be allowed to be a voting member of the Board .

provisions.title: Roles and functions of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The roles, functions and responsibilities of the Board shall be as follows:— (a) to ensure that the population receives appropriate and affordable promotive, preventive, curative and rehabilitative health care services (b) to discuss, amend health plans, budget and submit to the Council for approval; (c) to receive, analyse and approve implementation reports from the Council Health Management Team ; (d) to identify, mobilize and solicit financial resources for running council health services: (e) to mobilise edequate resources that will ensure effective, efficient and equitable access to health services in the District ; (f) to support Council Health Management Team in managing and administering health resources; (g) to prepare and recommend on a council wide health plan and budget the committee responsible for health services; (h) to promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable logistics and supply systems; (i) to develop, supervise and manage health services in the district; (j) to advise, recommend on human resources recruitment, training, selection, employment and Government Authorities.

provisions.title: Relationship of the Board with Community stakeholders and other authorities
provisions.content: Relationship of the Board with community, stakeholders and other authorities shall be as follows— (a) The Board shall ensure full involvementof the local community in priority identification, planning, financial contributions, monitoring and evaluation of health services delivery; (b) the Board shall be accountable to the Council whereby:— (i) The Council Health Plans, quarterly, annual technical and Financial reports shall be reviewed by Social Services Committee and Planning Committee and approved by full council; (ii) The Board shall conduct its activities in such a way that it shall maximize the efficiency of the Council and its existing organs without Council jeopardizing the Board 's autonomy. (c) the Council Health Management Team shall be a technical structure for implementing decision passed by the Board ; (d) in respect to Council Social Services Committee:— (i) all plans and progress reports shall pass. Therefore, this Committee will facilitate relationship; (ii) Close collaboration with other sectors will be actively searched by the Board towards the implementation of joint efforts in priority areas within the Council ; (e) with Non Governmental Organizations, private providers and donors:— (i) members representing the Voluntary Agency, Non Governmental Organizations and private providers in the Board will facilitate the relationship; (ii) the Board will requires proof from Council Health Management Team that other stakeholders have been involved in the planning process; (iii) the Baord shall solicit for funds from all various sources incuding Councils, Voluntary Agency, Community and donors. (g) the Regional Health Management Team shall provide technical support and advisory role; [Please note: numbering as in original.] (h) the Ministries responsible for Regional Administration Local Government and Health shall give technical support to the Board by formulating Policies, regulatory framework and monitoring of the Board 's activities.

provisions.title: Source and management of Funds
provisions.content: (1) The sources of funds for the Board shall be:— (a) Community contributions; (b) Community Health Funds; (c) Cost sharing and user charges fees; (d) Council revenue collection; (e) Donors and partners; (f) Central Government grants; (g) Other sources. (2) There shall be a single health services account to be known as account number 6. The account shall follow Local Government financial regulations, and signatories shall be as prescribed from time to time. (3) The Board shall maintain fund account for the purposes of Community Health Fund initiatives. The account will maintain revenues from sales of Council Health Fund cards and collection of user fees from Council Health facilities. The account will be used to contribute resources to Account No. 6 of the Council in financing health services. Signatories of this special account will be as stipulated in the by-law establishing Community Health Fund. (4) The Council shall appoint two accountants who shall oversee health Accounts under the Medical Officer of Health . (5) The Medical Officer of Health shall initiate and originate all payments. (6) Both accounts shall be audited following the prevailing Government regulations, (7) By-laws for Community Health Fund shall be formed for purposes of management of its activities in that particular area.

provisions.title: Establishment of Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: There shall be established for each district, the Council Health Management Team .

provisions.title: Compesition of the Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: The Council Health Management Team shall consist of:— (a) District Medical Officer; (b) District Health Secretary; (c) District Nursing Officer; (d) District Health Officer; (e) District Pharmacist; (f) District Medical Laboratory Technologist; (g) District Dental Surgeon.

provisions.title: Roles and function of the Council Health Management Team
provisions.content: The Roles and function of the Council Health Management Team shall be:— (a) to prepare comprehensive district health plans in line the district health needs and National District Health Planning Guidelines; (b) to ensure that hospitals, health centres, dispensaries implement health activities, community owned resource persons and the communities as per comprehensive district health plan; (c) to ensure the provision of transport, drugs, vaccines, medical supplies, equipment and reagentsto hospitals, health centres and dispensaries; (d) to respond to epidemics, emergencies and plan for control and preventive measures accordingly; (e) to carry out supportive supervision to district health staff at all levels in the district; (f) to ensure that quality health services are provided in line with national standards: (g) to ensure health legislation, regulations and professional ethics, and codes of conduct are adhered to in the district: (h) to ensure that data collection, analysis. utilisation and feedback are used by health workers to plan and ilupIement proper interventions: (i) to identify priority areas, organise and conduct operational research in the district; (j) to compile and analyse quarterly and annual all financial repolls and submit them to the Council Health Service Board and to the Regional Health Management Team ; (k) to organise meetings of all key health partners operating in the district in order to identifyand coordinate all health activities; (l) to organise and support outreach health services; (m) to monitor and evaluate implementation of health activities in the district;

provisions.title: Establishment of a Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) There shall be established for each Health Centre , a Health Centre Committee which shall be composed of: (a) three members appointed one each from a ward within the area; (b) one member appointed by the Council from the Dispensary Committee (on rotational basis); (c) one member appointed by private for profit health facility in the area; (d) the officer in-charge of the Health centre who shall be the Secretary ; (e) one member from the Ward Development Committee ; (f) one member representing private not for profit health facility. (2) Health Centre Committee may invite other persons who are not members ofthe Health Centre Committee to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons shall not take part in the final decisions of the committee. (3) At least one third of the members shall be women.

provisions.title: Qualification for membership
provisions.content: A person shall be qualified for appointment as a member of a Health Centre Committee if he or she satisfied the following conditions:— (a) he is a citizen of United Republic; (b) he has attained the age of twenty one year and not older than sixty five years; (c) he is ordinarily resident within the area; (d) the level of his education is primary school or above, and can read and write in Kiswahili; (e) he does not hold any political post.

provisions.title: Qualification for membership
provisions.content: (1) The term of office of a member shall be three years and all the members shall retire on the third anniversary of the date on which they assumed their officers and if not re-appointed are newly appointed members who shall come in office on that day shall fill their places. (2) Any appointed member may resign by giving a written notice of one month to the Chairman. (3) Where a member resigns or dies the vacant post shall be filled and the appointed member shall hold office only for the remainder of the term of his predecessor but shall be eligible for re-appointment. (4) if the Health Centre Committee fails to fulfil its expected obligations the council shall reprimand or dissolve the committee and arrange for an immediate selection. (5) The list of elected candidates shall be forwarded to ward Development Committee for final selection after being scrutinised by Ward Management Team.

provisions.title: Meetings of Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Committee shall hold ordinary meetings for the transactions of its business at least once in every three months at a place, dates and such times as it may decide provided that, the Committee may convene a special meetings as it may deem fit. (2) The Chairman of the Committee may convene a special meeting of the Committee after he receives a written request to that effect signed by not less than one third of all the members of the committee stating clearly the purposes for which the special meeting is to be convened. (3) The secretary shall, not less than tweny four hours before the time appointed for the holding of a meetingof a Committee, notify every rnember in writing of the place, day and time of the meeting and of the business proposed to be transacted at the meeting.

provisions.title: Functions of a Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) it shall be the duty of every Committee of a Health Centre :— (a) to receive and discuss implementation repolls prepared by the Health Centre Management Team ; (b) to liaise with the Council Health Board and other partnersin health provision and promotion in its area; (c) to promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable supply of drugs and logistic systems; (d) to advise and recommend to the Council Health Board on human resources development in terms of recruitment training deployment and motivation at the Health Centre level; (e) to facilitate the Health Centre Management Team in planning and managing community based health initiative within its catchment area in the context of the Ward, Development plan; (f) to promote availability of adequate Health Centre infrastructure; (g) to oversee the availability of assential supplies of drugs, reagents and medical equipment. (2) The Health Centre Committee shall be responsible and accountable to the Council for its day to day functions.

provisions.title: Funds and source of Revenue
provisions.content: (1) Funds and sources of revenue of a Health Centre shall constitute the following:— (a) community contributions; (b) donor or partner donations; (c) Government grants; (d) District Council grants; (e) any other source legally acquired. (2) There shall be a special account at a bank recognized by the Council where all the funds for a Helath Centre shall be deposited. This will be determined by the amount of funds in circulation. (3) There shall be two signatories for cheque of a Health Centre . The Officer in-charge of a Health Centre shall be category "A" and a user of a Health Centre shall be category "B". (4) There shall be in every Health Centre a strong room and safe for the custodian of money for centre. (5) The Health Centre account shall be audited in accordance with the Local Government Financial regulations. (6) The Health Centre shall send quarterly, biannual and annual progress reports to the Council Health Board to be represented before the full Council meetings.

provisions.title: Relations with other sectors
provisions.content: (1) The Health Centre Committee shall, in performance of its Functions:— (a) Make sure that the community needs are addressed; (b) Solicit funds from various sources; (c) Keep and maintain a Health Fund Cantributor's register; (d) Develop and strengthen partnership for collaboration, co-ordination and integration with other sectors in the areas; (e) Make sure that the Dispensary Committee is representedin the Health Centre Committee ; (f) Make sure that the Health Centre Committee is represented in the Council Health Service Board . (2) The Council Health Management team shall give technical advice to the Health Centre Management Team .

provisions.title: Establishment of Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: There shall be established a Ward Health Committee .

provisions.title: Composition Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: The Ward Health Committee shall comprise of:— (a) the Councilor of the respective ward; (b) The Ward Executive Officer; (c) One Head Teacher from a Primary School located in the Ward who shall be appointed by the Ward Development Committee ; (d) Two members from the community elected by the community of which, one of whom shall be a female; (e) A Clinical Officer or an assistant Clinical Officer in charge of health care facility who shall be the secretary to the committee; (f) one member appointed by the Ward Development Committee from amongst persons proposed by the villages within the area of that Ward; and (g) one representativefrom a community based organization appointed by the Ward Development Committee .

provisions.title: Functions of Ward Health Committee
provisions.content: Ward Health Committee shall have the following functions:— (a) to mobilize the Community to be members of the Community Health Fund; (b) to prepare the list of members and monitor the number of members in the community; (c) to supervise the collectors of annual contributions; (d) to monitor the level of contributions and user fee revenues; (e) to review Community Health Funds Operations make recommendations and take remedial actions; (f) to initiate and coordinate comprehensive community health plans; and (g) to organise general meetings of members of the Community Health Fund.

provisions.title: 26 . Establishment of a Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: (1) There is established a committee to be known as a Dispensary Committee . (2) A Dispensary Committee shall be composed of the following members:— (a) Selected members who shall be vote members as follows:— (i) Three members from community dispensary users; and (ii) One representative from private not for profit health facility; (iii) One member representing private for profit health facility. (b) Representing non-vote members as follows:— (i) One representative from a Ward Development Committee ; (ii) One representative from a Village Government Committee; and (iii) In-charge of the dispensary who shall be the Secretary. (3) Hospital Governing Committee may invite persons who are not their members to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons not take part in the final decisions of the Commitee. (4) At least ong third of the members shall be women.

provisions.title: Mode of selection and tenure of office
provisions.content: (1) Members of a Dispensary committee shall be selectedthrough the following procedure:— (a) Ward executive Director shall advertise the vacant post for representation of Health facilities users; (b) The list of elected candidates shall be forwarded to the Ward Management Team for scrutiny before being forwarded to Ward Development Committee which shall do the final Selection; (c) The final selection shall then be forwarded to the Council for approval and inform according to the Ward Development Committee . (2) Tenure of office for Dispensary Committee members shall be three years but may seek re-election for another term of three years only.

provisions.title: Meeting of Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: (1) A Dispensary Committee shall meet quarterly, butit may convene an extra-ordinary meeting when a need arise. (2) Fifty percent of the selected members together with a Chairperson or Secretary shall form a quorum for any meeting of a Dispensary Committee .

provisions.title: Qualification of members
provisions.content: A person shall be eligible to be a member of the Dispensary Commitee:— (a) He has attained the age of twenty one years-and not older than sixty five years; (b) He has completed primary education or above and know how to read and write Kiswahili; (c) He is a Tanzania and ordinarily a resident of a particular council. The condition of citizenship shall not apply to a person who is working in a voluntary health facility; (d) He is representing the users of a dispensary, and he is not be holding any political elected post through a political party; (e) He is mentally fit; (f) He has not been convicted of any criminal offence.

provisions.title: Functions of a Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: A Dispensary committee shall have the following functions:— (a) to ensure that the population in its area of jurisdiction receives appropriate and affordable health care services; (b) receive, discuss and pass the dispensary plans and budget; (c) receive the implementation report prepared by Dispensary Management Team; (d) identify and solicit financial resources for running dispensary services; (e) liaise with other health boards and partners in health provision and promotion; (f) promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable supply and logistic system; (g) advice and recommend on human resources concerning recruitment, training, selection and deployment to relevant authorities; (h) assist and facilitate the Dispensary Management Team in planning and managing the community based health initiatives within its catchment area in the context of the Ward Development Plan; (i) Submit quarterly, biannual and annual reports to the Council Health Board ; and (j) do any relevant activity as may be directed by the Ward Devel of Committee.

provisions.title: Relationship of Dispansary Committee with relevant Stakeholders
provisions.content: For the better performance and effeciency in its day to day activities; a Dispensary committee shall liaise with the following:— (a) community by ensuring that the community contributre members to its committee to ensure that community needs are adequately addressed arid facilitate flow of information from both ways; (b) Council Health Management Team which shall give technical advice to it; (c) The Council Health Service Boardby being represented in it on a rotational basis to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (d) The Dispensary Management Team to ensure that the team implements Council 's decisions; (e) The Health Centre Committee by being represented in order to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (f) Other stakeholders by soliciting funds, develop and strengthen partnership for collaboration, co-ordination and integration.

provisions.title: Sources of funds
provisions.content: Sources of funds for a Dispensary Committee shall include but not limited to the following:— (i) community contribution; (ii) CHF; (iii) Council funds; (iv) funds from donors or partners; (iv) Central Government; and [Please note: numbering as in original.] (vi) other sources legally acquired.
